entry.title: Fake Bahrain Alert App Deploys Android Surveillance Malware
entry.content:
A malicious application delivers four-stage Android spyware via phony Google Play sites, exploiting civilian fear during Iranian missile strikes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain
entry.content:
Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Flaws in Passkey Implementation Show Old Attacks Still Work
entry.content:
Ahead of Black Hat USA, researchers find exploitable flaws in how Microsoft handles passkeys that could allow attackers to impersonate privileged users.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Attack Puts a Chill on Japanese Frozen-Food Chain
entry.content:
A cyberattack on a food and logistics firm disrupts the supply of frozen food to thousands of clients, including major franchises like Kentucky Fried Chicken.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russian Hackers Exploit Zimbra Zero-Day Against US, Ukraine Targets
entry.content:
A state-sponsored threat group, dubbed "Laundry Bear," sends "half-click" phishing emails that require a victim only to open or preview the message.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Europe's Multilingual Reality Exposes AI Security Gaps
entry.content:
The AI security layer and guardrails for many AI products don't evenly protect against jailbreaking and unsafe actions in every single language.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: US Warns of Iranian Hackers Targeting Siemens, Schneider, and Rockwell ICS Devices
entry.content:
An updated advisory from federal agencies provides information on the techniques used to hack programmable logic controllers. The post US Warns of Iranian Hackers Targeting Siemens, Schneider, and Rockwell ICS Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
entry.content:
The vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-16232 has been exploited against customers with certain configurations. The post New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses
entry.content:
Hackers recently obtained non-sensitive customer information and other documents from the company. The post Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Nuclear-Sabotage Malware Benchmark Trips Up Most Frontier AI Models
entry.content:
SentinelOne’s new benchmark, built on the Fast16 case, shows which AI models can sustain a malware investigation and which cannot. The post Nuclear-Sabotage Malware Benchmark Trips Up Most Frontier AI Models appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Abstract Raises $25 Million to Expand Composable Security Operations Platform
entry.content:
The latest investment round brings the total raised by Abstract to nearly $50 million. The post Abstract Raises $25 Million to Expand Composable Security Operations Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack
entry.content:
Threat actors used credentials obtained from other companies to hack into Chick-fil-A One accounts. The post Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Is Patching Dead? Vulnerability Management in the Post-Mythos Era
entry.content:
You cannot out-patch a machine that writes a working exploit from a vulnerability description in twenty hours. Stop trying to optimize a game you cannot win. The post Is Patching Dead? Vulnerability Management in the Post-Mythos Era appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider
entry.content:
AgentForger allows an attacker to create, insert and remotely control an invisible autonomous AI agent inside a victim organization. The post OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Data Breach Confirmed After Australian Energy Giant Origin Is Hacked
entry.content:
A hacker claims to have stolen the information of 2 million Origin Energy customers and is threatening to leak it. The post Data Breach Confirmed After Australian Energy Giant Origin Is Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Swiss train maker Stadler refuses Everest $12 million ransomware demand
entry.content:
Stadler Rail said it will not make a $12.3 million ransom payment after cybercriminals stole technical data from a supplier's file-sharing platform.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Major Australian energy supplier confirms customer data compromised
entry.content:
Origin Energy said it was working to figure out how many Australians were affected by a recent data breach.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: State Department imposes visa restrictions on foreign cyber scammers
entry.content:
Individuals connected to transnational cyber-scam operations face U.S. visa restrictions under a new policy announced by Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: International alert spotlights Russia-linked attacks on Zimbra webmail
entry.content:
A Kremlin-backed group known as Laundry Bear has been using a zero-click phishing technique to break into Zimbra webmail accounts worldwide, the U.S. and other nations said.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
entry.content:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
entry.content:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
entry.content:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
entry.content:
A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
entry.content:
The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
entry.content:
Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ApolloMD Agrees to Pay $4.02M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
ApolloMD Business Services, a business associate that provides integrated, multispecialty physician, APC, and practice management services, has agreed to settle […] The post ApolloMD Agrees to Pay $4.02M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Major Healthcare Software Vendor Investigating Cyberattack
entry.content:
The healthcare technology company Craneware is investigating a cybersecurity incident and has confirmed that a significant amount of data was […] The post Major Healthcare Software Vendor Investigating Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: $3 Million Settlement Agreed to Resolve Healthcare Services Group Data Breach Litigation
entry.content:
Healthcare Services Group has agreed to pay $3,000,000 to settle litigation arising from a September 2024 cybersecurity incident that involved […] The post $3 Million Settlement Agreed to Resolve Healthcare Services Group Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: TriWest Healthcare Alliance Announced Breach Affecting Almost 12,000 Tricare Beneficiaries
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by TriWest Healthcare Alliance, Texas Medicaid and Healthcare Partnership, the Minnesota Health Insurance Network, and […] The post TriWest Healthcare Alliance Announced Breach Affecting Almost 12,000 Tricare Beneficiaries appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Patient Data Exposed in Cybersecurity Incident at Ohio Revenue Cycle Management Company
entry.content:
Security incidents have resulted in the exposure of patient data at United Technology Systems, Meridian Health Plan of Illinois, and […] The post Patient Data Exposed in Cybersecurity Incident at Ohio Revenue Cycle Management Company appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Heart Care Centers of Illinois Discovers Historic Phishing Attack Exposed Patient Data
entry.content:
The cardiovascular medical practice, Heart Care Centers of Illinois (HCCI), announced on July 18, 2026, that certain patients had some […] The post Heart Care Centers of Illinois Discovers Historic Phishing Attack Exposed Patient Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Colorado Behavioral Healthcare Provider Discovers Insider Data Breach
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by NAS Recovery Solutions, Entyre Care Massachusetts, Carle Health, and Brown Health Medical Group-MA. NAS […] The post Colorado Behavioral Healthcare Provider Discovers Insider Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Report Shows Surge in Malicious Insider Incidents; Mega Data Breaches
entry.content:
There has been a general trend of increasing data breaches over the past decade, with this year on track to […] The post Report Shows Surge in Malicious Insider Incidents; Mega Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
entry.content:
South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
entry.content:
The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Check Point warns of SmartConsole zero-day exploited in attacks
entry.content:
Israeli cybersecurity firm Check Point Software has addressed an actively exploited zero-day flaw in the company's SmartConsole graphical user interface (GUI) admin panel. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
entry.content:
A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware
entry.content:
Ukraine's CERT has uncovered attacks distributing an archive containing the legitimate Notepad++ application and a malicious utility called LunchPoke disguised as a plugin to establish persistence. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra zero-click flaw for email theft
entry.content:
CISA is warning that the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Laundry Bear, also known as Void Blizzard, is targeting organizations using Zimbra Collaboration email servers by combining phishing attacks with the exploitation of a now-patched Zimbra vulnerability. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Australian energy provider Origin says data breach exposes client data
entry.content:
Origin Energy has confirmed that an unauthorized party accessed and subsequently leaked customer data online, exposing sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), among others. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Clop ransomware targets Windchill, FlexPLM in data theft attacks
entry.content:
The Clop ransomware gang (also tracked as Cl0p) is targeting Internet-exposed PTC Windchill and FlexPLM instances in a new data theft extortion campaign. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Omnicell Report Victim omnicell.com Threat Actor Everest Date Discovered Jul 23, 2026 Description Omnicell is a healthcare technology company based in the USA (Pennsylvania), â¦July 23, 2026
entry.content:
Omnicell Report Victim omnicell.com Threat Actor Everest Date Discovered Jul 23, 2026 Description Omnicell is a healthcare technology company based in the USA (Pennsylvania), â¦July 23, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to Everest.

entry.title: Personal data of all South Korean diplomats believed leaked in ‘unprecedented’ cyberattack
entry.content:
Seo Ji-Eun reports: The personal information of nearly all of South Korea’s diplomatic personnel is presumed to have been compromised in what the Foreign Ministry on Tuesday called an “unprecedented” cyberattack, exposing up to 10,000 administrative and intelligence records. The data system of the Korea National Diplomatic Academy (KNDA) — an institution affiliated with the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Cyberattack against Maine telecom disrupted municipal internet service in 23 towns
entry.content:
Colin Wood reports: At least one municipal government was among those to see their internet service disrupted after a cyberattack against a Maine telecommunications firm Sunday caused an outage affecting 23 towns along the state’s midcoastal region. A local NBC affiliate reported that the government office of Damariscotta, a tourist town of roughly 2,300 inhabitants, known for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices.
entry.content:
Hannah Murphy reports: Nearly half of companies that are targets of a ransomware cyber attack end up paying a ransom to release their data or systems, according to 2025 research from cybersecurity group Sophos, while the median amount demanded is rising. Globally, some jurisdictions are responding by banning payments to hackers. In the UK, for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Milford, New Hampshire Confirms Unauthorized Activity, Withholds Details of Suspected Cyberattack (1)
entry.content:
Milford, New Hampshire is a quintessential New England town. But charm is no defense against cyberattackers, and it appears that the town may have been attacked last week. As DysruptionHub was the first to report, the town began experiencing problems early on July 15. Town email alerts, shared with DataBreaches by a town resident, reveal... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Greedy ransomware crews return for seconds after victims cough up first extortion payments
entry.content:
Connor Jones reports: Authorities have long warned organizations not to pay ransoms, and fresh figures underline why: handing over the money doesn’t mean the crooks leave you alone. Proofpoint survey data suggests that 58 percent of affected UK organizations paid a ransom. Worse, 22 percent of those who pay get extorted again anyway. The UK... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
entry.content:
It’s happened. The nightmare of the future is now in our present. Or was this just old-fashioned negligence? Lily Hay Newman and Dell Cameron report: OpenAI disclosed on Tuesday that it lost control of two AI models during a security test that ended in a breach of the open AI research platform Hugging Face. Describing the incident as... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Instructure Incident Driving 58 Percent of Breach Notices in 2026
entry.content:
GovTech reports: The mega breach is back in 2026, according to a new report from the Identity Theft Resource Center (ITRC). The nonprofit group, which works to prevent and reduce incidences of identify theft, found that 1,029 data compromises generated 471 million breach notices in the first half of the year, with one incident —... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: TN: Data breach delays start of Sumner County school year
entry.content:
Camellia Burris reports: One Middle Tennessee school district is delaying the start of the school year due to a data breach in its computer network. School officials in Sumner County discovered the breach in its computer network earlier this week and subsequently revised the district calendar so the problem could be resolved before students return... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: ID: Kootenai County notifies residents of data breach
entry.content:
Nick Hawthorne reports: Kootenai County has begun notifying residents whose personal information may have been compromised in a ransomware attack detected on the county’s computer network in late March. According to a Kootenai County press release, the County discovered the ransomware on March 30, 2026, and immediately took action to secure its network and restore... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Iranian-Affiliated Cyber Actors Exploit Programmable Logic Controllers Across US Critical Infrastructure
entry.content:
Publication: April 7, 2026 Last Update: July 22, 2026 TLP: Clear From the updated version of the Joint Cybersecurity Advisory: Executive Summary The authoring agencies urgently warn U.S. organizations of ongoing Iranian-affiliated cyber targeting of internet-connected operational technology (OT) devices, including programmable logic controllers (PLCs). These actions disrupted PLCs across several U.S. critical infrastructure sectors... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Xsolis, Inc.
entry.content:
